<!-- gate_claim: paste gate_claim here -->
gate_claim: the receipt diet is correct and finalize-gate-safe with the two epoch-1 review-gate defects repaired: (R1) the diff-scope classifier now recognizes root cross-edition READ surfaces that a non-claude chain's contract validator asserts byte-parity on (e.g. commands/workflow-init.md asserted by the codex-chain validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js, and .agents/plugins/marketplace.json), so any diff touching such a root path fails closed to all four chains and never yields a false-green claude-only finalize receipt, and the fix closes the CLASS of root read surfaces not merely the two reported files; the classifier stays fail-closed by construction (unresolved base or git error -> all four) and records the scope decision plus touched-path diff evidence in the receipt; (R2) the KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS bound is enforced per-CHAIN wall-clock as documented in the run-chains header (a decomposed multi-step chain whose cumulative wall-clock passes the bound is killed and marked timed_out), not silently widened to steps-times-timeout, and the header doc matches the code; every executed step still records a per-step wall-clock duration; the receipt stays strictly ADDITIVE so the existing chains[] verdict contract (name, exitCode, accepted_red, headSha, workTreeHash) that plan-validator --finalize-check, release-check, and the walkthrough/test-release synthetic fixtures read is unchanged; release.js --release-check still requires an unconditional all-four receipt; each hoisted cross-chain repeat runs exactly once while every chain stays individually runnable standalone; the #547 code-tree freshness hash still validates a scoped receipt; all four run-chains editions stay in parity (byte-identical claude<->codex, rename-normalized gitlab/gitea) with all four chains green; the >=50% common-case cut is NOT a blocking clause of this gate — it is re-attributed to Phase E of the epic per the recorded user decision (kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/acb-decision.md) with the honestly-measured ~20% common-case cut recorded and carried forward as a deferred follow-up; and the CLAUDE.md/CHANGELOG documentation delta describes only shipped behavior with no provenance leakage and CLAUDE.md under 200 lines
<!-- gate_surface: paste gate_surface here -->
gate_surface: the full candidate: the run-chains four-edition family (canonical scripts/ copy, the codex byte twin under plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/, the gitlab and gitea rename-normalized ports), its test surfaces (test-run-chains.js and the two forge test ports test-gitlab-run-chains.js / test-gitea-run-chains.js), and the n2 documentation delta (CLAUDE.md Validation Policy + CHANGELOG), reviewed against the accumulated diff vs the claim root base f92ec240 and n1's before/after timing evidence, with particular attention to the R1 diff-scope classifier coverage of root cross-edition read surfaces and the R2 per-chain timeout enforcement

Context echo (from the canonical review context card, read first): plan_schema_version 2, contract_version 2, plan_hash d39f89af03fab7a5511d2cdf7fb97b7c719162e87b47b896e5a9dc51c77384cd, epoch field 1 with epoch_lineage_id a51a6b4bd9b931c3d59f860bc99d07b68a1fa826583a70fb922843cf123bac12, attempt_ordinal 1, review_phase discovery, gate_mode change_gate, claim_root_base commit f92ec240e3a02c3e8b10d07ebac7e3a9f4a3f088, logical_gate key 3fc0f0687172272ebaf4925a6dbc3fe711cf0b06c24fb341f3b58e86a1d8d0e0, inherited_frontier classes [code] digest 54f4cd3cd9cfc8c8c097f64f4aa18f3fca89b9bda16acca6425e3bbc9b9c46a2, prior_findings [], validation_obligations [], repair_delta null. Upstream evidence c1-scope-timeout-fix.md (nonce 2b57aba7d890, RED and GREEN records), the epoch-1 producer evidence n1-receipt-diet.md (nonce 8176ed345212, BEFORE/AFTER timing table) and prior gate n3-code-review.md (archived under .cache/epochs/1/files/.cache/), and the user decision record acb-decision.md were all read before review.

Candidate inspected: worktree HEAD equals the claim root base f92ec240; the candidate is the uncommitted accumulated diff confined to the frozen surface (scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js, its codex byte twin, the gitlab/gitea rename-normalized ports, scripts/test-run-chains.js, the two forge test ports, CLAUDE.md, CHANGELOG.md; 9 files, 2167 insertions / 304 deletions) plus untracked run evidence under kaola-workflow/issue-725/. No out-of-surface writes. Certifier binding independently verified: recomputing plan-validator computeCodeTreeHash over this tree reproduces the seeded certified_candidate_digest exactly (158b43a697cb88a074ca94410adbbeb0345996855c5548917d1dab23e9e9d446), so the reviewed candidate is the one bound at open time.

R1 verified by re-execution, not prose. (a) Independent probe suite (scratchpad, driving the exported classifyScope of this worktree against fresh scratch git repos): a diff touching commands/workflow-init.md and one touching .agents/plugins/marketplace.json each select all four chains with reason edition_coupling and the path recorded in touchedEditionPaths; class spot-checks BEYOND the reported pair and beyond the T38 fixture list also force all four: commands/brand-new-command.md, agents/new-role.md, .agents/profiles/new-profile.toml, docs/conventions.md, docs/decisions/D-999-01.md, CLAUDE.md, install.sh, uninstall.sh, and the codex-mirrored scripts/kaola-workflow-validation-runner.js (filesystem-existence arm). Over-capture guards: src/feature.js, the nested src/commands/x.md, and an unmirrored scripts/local-only-helper.js all still narrow to claude-only / non_edition_diff. Fail-closed arms: an unresolvable KAOLA_FINALIZE_BASE and a non-git cwd both yield all-four / base_unresolved. 16/16 probes pass. (b) Class closure independently audited from the chain definitions: the scripts exclusive to non-claude chains are validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js, the two forge contract validators, edition-sync --check, and the codex/forge walkthroughs; grepping their root reads yields CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/workflow-state-contract.md, docs/api.md, commands/workflow-init.md, commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md, agents/workflow-planner.md, .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, install.sh, uninstall.sh, package.json, and scripts/kaola-workflow-validation-runner.js - every one covered by ROOT_EDITION_READ_PREFIXES (.agents/, commands/, agents/, docs/), ROOT_EDITION_READ_FILES (CLAUDE.md, README.md, install.sh, uninstall.sh), the package.json arm, or the codex-mirror existence arm; edition-sync mirror-set members are all root scripts/*.js with codex same-name counterparts, so the existence arm covers that surface too. The prefix entries over-approximate their class, so newly added files in those directories are covered without a list edit - fail-closed by construction. (c) Candidate test suites re-run green here: test-run-chains.js 233 assertions (T38 all 10 root read surfaces, T38b claude-only preserved), gitlab port 39, gitea port 39 (G7/G8).

R2 verified by re-execution. Independent probe (real CLI, scratch repo, 4 steps x 300ms sleep under KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS=700): the chain is killed once cumulative wall-clock passes the per-chain bound - steps 1-2 green (345ms + 347ms = 692ms), step 3 killed after 12ms with timed_out true and SIGTERM, chain exitCode 1, CLI wall 795ms (never steps x timeout); a 2x200ms chain under a 30000ms budget still completes green with per-step durations recorded. T39 (canonical) and G8 (both forge ports) pass. The header doc (KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS per-chain timeout in ms) now matches the code: runChainSteps records a chainStart origin and passes each step spawn only the remaining budget, with a synthetic timed-out kill when the budget is already spent. Transient retry inside one step still grants the retry attempt the step-start remaining budget - the pre-existing per-attempt retry semantics from the base (unchanged by this candidate; a timeout kill remains non-retryable), so not a candidate defect.

Accepted mechanism re-confirmed intact: (1) per-step timings - every executed step recorded as command/duration_ms/exitCode in chains[].steps[] and preamble.steps[] (probes + T32/G4). (2) Receipt strictly additive - chains[] rows keep name/exitCode/duration_ms/accepted_red/accepted_red_issue/attempts/retried_transient/timed_out/signal; additions are steps[], optional preamble_failed, and top-level scope + preamble; plan-validator, release.js, and package.json are untouched by the diff, and all readers (finalize-check, release-check, walkthrough + test-release synthetic fixtures) ran green under the full npm test. (3) release.js release-check still unconditional all-four: the release-check precedence chains_incomplete then chains_red then chains_waived is intact in the untouched plan-validator (subset receipts refuse chains_incomplete), and the bare (no --project/--plan) run stays unscoped (T36; reason no_project_context). (4) Hoist - T35 proves the shared step executes exactly once (exec-count) and is attributed to receipt.preamble; a preamble failure marks every containing chain red (never a false green); package.json is not in the diff so every chain stays standalone-runnable (and the full npm test ran each chain green). (5) The #547 code-tree freshness hash is chain-selection-independent: codeTreeHash stamping and the finalize-check comparison are both outside the diff, and the walkthrough (which exercises the finalize gate against produced receipts) is green; my computeCodeTreeHash recompute over this tree also matches the open-time value. (6) Four-edition parity: cmp proves the codex twin byte-identical; validate-script-sync and edition-sync --check green; the gitlab/gitea ports carry the R1 constants and the R2 chainStart/remaining logic under rename normalization with their forge classifier require intact. (7) All four chains green: npm test (claude, codex, gitlab, gitea, sequential) exit 0 in this worktree on this candidate.

Docs delta: CLAUDE.md is 143 lines (under 200); the single added Validation Policy bullet describes only shipped behavior (finalize-context predicate, claude-only vs all-four vs fail-closed, release exemption) and carries no provenance (the two D-725-01 references elsewhere in CLAUDE.md are pre-existing at the base). The CHANGELOG entry describes the shipped steps[]/scope/preamble receipt, names the honest roughly-20% common-case cut, makes no 50% claim, and keeps the issue reference in its proper CHANGELOG home.

The >=50% common-case cut clause: per the frozen gate_claim and the recorded user decision (acb-decision.md), this is NOT a blocking clause of this gate - the target is re-attributed to Phase E of the epic. The residual shortfall (honestly measured ~20% serial / ~0% concurrent common-case cut, from n1 evidence) is carried forward as prose context only - it is already durably recorded in acb-decision.md, run-gaps-manual.md, and the c3-finalize brief, whose Run-gaps sweep mandate carries it; every other clause verdict above is my own.

Deferral carry-forward context (prose record only; no structured finding row is emitted because an approved code-review settles with zero non-resolved structured findings - the canonical one-line deferral descriptions prepared for the record are kept below as narrative context):
R3 precondition: the epic-level >=50 percent common-case wall-clock cut target for the receipt diet, re-attributed to Phase E of the epic by the recorded user decision in kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/acb-decision.md, with the Phase B mechanism accepted as landed
R3 input: n1 instrument-first measurements: claude 696.5s, codex 17.2s, gitlab 79.8s, gitea 76.8s, all-four serial 870.3s
R3 expected: a recorded common-case wall-clock cut of at least 50 percent
R3 observed: a recorded common-case cut of 20.0 percent serial and about 0 percent concurrent, carried forward as a deferred follow-up to Phase E per the user decision, with the finalize Run-gaps sweep carrying the gap

verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
review_summary: no_blocking_findings
review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: The epoch-2 repair closes both epoch-1 defects for the whole class, not just the reported files: the diff-scope classifier now forces all four chains for every root cross-edition read surface the non-claude validators assert on while a genuinely claude-only diff still narrows, verified by sixteen independent probes plus the 233-assertion suite, and the per-chain timeout bound is enforced as documented, killing a multi-step chain mid-run at its cumulative budget; the additive receipt, release-path strictness, hoist exactly-once behavior, freshness hash, four-edition parity, and docs delta all re-verified intact with all four chains green, leaving only the user-deferred 50 percent target carried forward as a non-blocking follow-up.
